commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=58178051cb11fed82f04477eea6467793bf31f12)
commands.review_summary:
The PR delays Android `GatewaySession.currentConnection` publication until the gateway connect RPC succeeds and adds a bounded notification-event queue with reconnect reconciliation tests.

PR surface: Other +431. Total +431 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main publishes `currentConnection` before `conn.connect()` completes, and notification forwarding sends `notifications.changed` through `sendNodeEvent` without a retry path. I did not run a live Android/gateway repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed because the branch has a compile-breaking test fixture and lacks real behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's external setup evidence.

target.title: fix(android): queue node events until gateway connect
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #79552.

## Problem

Android notification forwarding could send `node.event` before the gateway websocket `connect` request completed. The gateway rejects any first frame that is not `connect`, and Android did not retry the failed notification event, so notification-triggered automations could miss events during reconnect.

## Root Cause

`GatewaySession` exposed `currentConnection` before the connect handshake finished. `NodeRuntime` treated a non-null connection as ready and sent notification events directly, while the notification listener had no bounded retry or reconnect reconciliation path.

## Solution

- Publish `currentConnection` only after the gateway connect RPC succeeds.
- Keep reconnect/disconnect cleanup aware of both connecting and connected sockets.
- Queue notification events in a bounded FIFO while delivery is unavailable.
- Flush queued events after reconnect and reconcile active Android notifications that were posted but not delivered.
- Track delivered posted notifications to avoid duplicate reconnect replays.

## Testing Performed

- `git diff --check` passed.
- Attempted Android focused test:
  - `./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.gateway.GatewaySessionInvokeTest`
  - Blocked locally because `JAVA_HOME` is not set and no `java` command is available.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Android `node.event` notification events no longer use a connection until the gateway connect handshake succeeds, and reconnect windows keep notification events queued for later delivery.

Real environment tested: Source-level Android unit-test path in this checkout; local Android runtime tests could not execute because Java is unavailable in the environment.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check`; `./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.gateway.GatewaySessionInvokeTest`.

Evidence after fix: `git diff --check` passed. Gradle reported `JAVA_HOME is not set and no 'java' command could be found in your PATH`, so Android test execution was blocked before running tests.

Observed result after fix: Source changes gate `currentConnection` publication behind successful connect and add bounded notification queue/reconnect reconciliation coverage in Android tests.

What was not tested: Live Android device reconnect, live gateway notification forwarding, and Android Gradle unit execution in this environment due missing Java.
target.author: ashishpatel26
